title: Spiritual Beast Guide - Type, Buff, Enhance
summary: 20 spiritual beasts (support gear on a general); 3 generations; the monster-hunt beast is Bird of Hurricane (Mounted + Double Monster Drop +26%).
content: Spiritual Beast = support gear assigned to a general to boost its buffs; 20 types, each specialized to one troop type. Buffs listed are at MAX (Lv20, Quality Red). 3 generations: 1st = no debuff, 2nd = 1 debuff, 3rd = 2 debuffs. Rule of thumb: put 2nd/3rd-gen (debuff) beasts on Subordinate-City MAYORS (their debuff works when the sub-city joins battle; the buff does not reach the main army). 1st gen (no debuff, unlocked by Pasture level): Thunder Scorpion (Pasture Lv6, Ranged Atk/Def/HP +52%, March Speed +52%); Bird of Hurricane (Lv11, MOUNTED Atk/Def/HP +52% + DOUBLE ITEMS DROP FROM MONSTERS +26% -- the monster-hunting beast, put it on the mounted hunting general; few other double-drop sources exist); Lord of Lava (Lv16, Ground Atk/Def/HP +52% + Troop Load +52%, for RSS plunder); Behemoth King (Lv22, Siege Atk/Def/HP +52% + March Size +26%). 2nd gen (each +78 to +130% + one -52% enemy debuff): Pegasus, Nine-tailed Fox, Siren, Kong, Burning Godzilla (wall), Chiron, Thunderbird, Chimera, Nandi (wall). 3rd gen (Hati, Jormungandr, Otso, Duneyrr, Chrysomallos, Rainbow Crow, Tarasque): +104% buffs + a -52% debuff + a 'when defending' -104% debuff. Attaching a beast to an ASSISTANT/DUTY OFFICER gives no buff/debuff (only raises power for duty-officer power requirements); on a MAYOR the debuff is significant if the sub-city joins. Activate via Pasture (built at keep 21), 300,000 Spiritual Beast EXP per beast; 2nd/3rd gen also need an Unlocking Item bought with Spiritual Beast Scale (2nd = 400 scales, 3rd = 800). Spiritual Beast Scale drops from bosses, cap 42/day (drops from normal bosses Lv1 Zombie up, and event Lava Turtle/Cerberus/Bayard; NOT Viking/Ymir). Beasts are near-useless untrained (+1-4%). Train via Level Up (EXP) and Smelt (Quality up: gems/medals or smelt items + gold; Awakening Stone needed Gold->Red). Level EXP: unlock->Lv10 = 12.5M, Lv10->20 = 90M, unlock->Lv20 = 102.5M cumulative. Quality must reach Red before Lv11+. Dragon vs Beast: dragons are stronger at full development but far more expensive -- early game develop and use beasts first, hold dragons until general equipment is sufficient.

title: Dragon Complete Guide - Type, Buff, Enhance
summary: 14 dragons (support gear, stronger than beasts at max but costly); FASOLT is the dedicated Boss-Hunt (Mounted) dragon with a Mounted-Attack-on-Monsters talent.
content: Dragon = support gear on a general; a well-developed dragon adds +100 to attributes and larger buffs than a beast (theoretical +60-125% vs a beast's ~+30-52%). 14 dragons in 3 classes: Pasture (Celtic Demon, Jawzahr, Fafnir, Fasolt, Radiantwyrm), Sacred (Norway Ridge, Thebes, Nidhogg, Ladon, Amaterasu, Colchian, Ziz, Yggdrasil), Elder (Gorynych). Primary role by dragon: Celtic Demon=Ranged; Jawzahr=Ranged/Mounted/Defense; Fafnir=Mounted/Siege; FASOLT=BOSS HUNT (Mounted), Ground -- its Talent II is 'Mounted Troop Attack on Monsters' (an against-monster buff), making it THE monster-hunting dragon; Radiantwyrm/Ziz/Nidhogg=Mounted; Norway Ridge/Amaterasu=Ground; Thebes/Yggdrasil=Wall Defender; Ladon/Gorynych=Ranged; Colchian=Siege. For a mounted boss-hunter: Fasolt is the goal dragon; before that use the Bird of Hurricane beast. Recommended monster-hunt mounted dragon at every spend tier = Fasolt. Attaching a dragon to an Assistant/Duty Officer gives no buff (only power); on a Mayor the debuff works if the sub-city joins. Some generals only trigger skills with a dragon and may specify the type (e.g. Isabella I needs a Sacred Dragon). How to get: Celtic Demon = Pasture Lv26 + 300,000 gems; Fafnir = Pasture Lv30 + 1,400 Dragon Crystals; Fasolt/Jawzahr/Radiantwyrm = Dragon Pattern (Wheel of Dragon War, ~$340/dragon); Sacred/Elder from Wonder levels + All-Star Battlefield ranking eggs. Train by Refine FIRST (cheap big buffs, e.g. Lv1 Celtic Demon ~+60% attack), then Level (feeding: resources/gold/gems/materials/refining stones), then Talent. Hold off leveling dragons until general equipment is developed.

title: Dragon Cliff
summary: Building (keep 30) that levels/refines Sacred dragons and unlocks their 3rd (active-skill) talents; not directly a monster-hunt tool.
content: Dragon Cliff unlocks at keep level 30; upgraded via Victory Column + Triumphal Arch levels. It levels up and refines Sacred/Wonder-unlocked dragons and unlocks their Active Skill (the 3rd talent). Milestones: Lv1 Norway Ridge active skill; Lv2 Thebes; Lv3 Nidhogg; Lv4 Ladon; Lv5 Amaterasu/Colchian/Ziz. No resource cost for the cliff itself (0 food/lumber/stone/ore), only keep + prerequisite building levels. Not directly relevant to solo monster hunting; relevant only if a Sacred dragon is your hunter's dragon.

title: How to get Spiritual Beast EXP efficiently
summary: Beast EXP sources: high-level resource tiles, World Boss ranking, killing bosses (normal L5-L13 and event L1-5 = up to 5,000 each), Relics Chamber (100k), Black Market.
content: Spiritual Beast EXP (300,000 needed to activate one beast) sources: Resource tiles (luck): tile 11-12 = 5,000 EXP, tile 8-10 = 1,000, tile 4-7 = 500; none from tiles 1-3/13-15. World Boss single-hit-damage ranking: top1 = 100,000 EXP, 2-5 = 50k, 6-9 = 40k, 10-20 = 30k, 21-49 = 20k, 50-98 = 10k, 99-100 = 5k. Kill bosses (luck): normal Werewolf L5/Manticore L6 = 1,000; Yasha L7/Peryton L8 = 2,000; Minotaur L9 through Fafnir L13 = 5,000. NONE from Zombie L1-Skeleton L4 or from Behemoth L14/Phoenix L15/Jormungandr L16/Typhon L17. Event bosses: Royal Thief = 2,000; Lava Turtle/Golem/Witch/Warlord L1-5 = 5,000; Hydra L1-4 = 5,000; Cerberus L1-3 = 5,000; Ymir/Nian L1-5 = 5,000; Garuda L1/2/3 = 15k/30k/40k. Not from Fenrir, Bayard, Viking, Pumpkin. Relics Chamber (luck) = 100,000. Black Market = 1,000 EXP x5 for 1,000 gems, x10 for 2,000 gems. Bot note: farming mid normal bosses (Minotaur L9-Fafnir L13) doubles as steady beast-EXP income.

title: Boss / Monster Power List
summary: Full power ladder: Normal bosses B1-B23, event-boss ladders per type, Viking Easy/Normal/Hard/Hell tiers, and normal monsters Lv1-50. Guides publish Power only (difficulty proxy).
content: Normal Boss ladder (B# = map boss level, Power): B1 Zombie 36.5K, B2 Redcap 219K, B3 Centaur 328.5K, B4 Skeleton Dragon 787.2K, B5 Werewolf 1.3M, B6 Manticore 2.2M, B7 Yasha 3.4M, B8 Peryton 6.5M, B9 Minotaur 9.9M, B10 Griffin 17.9M, B11 Ifrit 59.6M, B12 Kamaitachi 89.4M, B13 Fafnir 134.1M, B14 Behemoth 187.7M, B15 Phoenix 262.7M, B16 Jormungandr 394.1M, B17 Typhon 551.8M, B18 Ammit 800.1M, B19 Stymphalian Bird 1.1B, B20 Kraken 1.6B, B21 Azazel 2.6B, B22 Leviathan 3.3B, B23 Garmr 4.7B. Event-boss ladders (Power by level): Ymir L1-7 = 23.4M/70.8M/120.2M/206.4M/361.3M/722.6M/1.8B; Cerberus L1-5 = 68.2M/153.4M/234.2M/694.9M/1.5B; Pan L1-7 = 22.3M/42.8M/122.9M/206.4M/361.3M/711.7M/1.8B; Hydra L1-6 = 84.9M/120.6M/198.9M/328.4M/678.8M/1.5B; Sphinx L1-8 = 12.4M/22.3M/74.5M/147.5M/225.2M/472.9M/914.4M/2B; Witch/Warlord L1-7 = 13M/24.6M/85.6M/154.8M/225.2M/668.1M/1.5B; Golem/Lava Turtle L1-7 = 12.4M/22.3M/74.5M/147.5M/225.2M/668.1M/1.5B; Bayard L1-5 = 65.5M/147.5M/225.2M/668.1M/1.5B; Nian L1-6 = 59.6M/71.5M/89.4M/134.1M/236.5M/472.9M; Royal Thief L1 = 13.6M; Garuda L1-3 = 1.3M/1.7M/125M; Fenrir L1-3 = 27.4K/186.1K/492.7K; Taotie L1-3 = 124.6M/398.1M/805.1M; Desert Bandit 1-10 = 3.5M..289M. Viking tiers by difficulty (Lv1-50): Easy 1.1M-14.1M; Normal 3.2M-91.7M; Hard 10.6M-196.9M; Hell 17.9M-760.1M; Alliance-Challenge Icebreaker/Berserker/Chief/King = 89.4M/134.1M/262.7M/1B. Normal monsters (map-tile mobs, not bosses) Lv1-50: Robber -> ... -> Kheshig L41-45 (6M-34.5M) -> Takeda Fire Cavalry L46-50 (53.4M-308.2M); plus Goblins 200K-500K. Guides publish Power only, no HP; treat Power as a difficulty proxy. Site updates the ladder as new bosses ship (latest B23 Garmr, Taotie).

title: Boss Chest & Drop Item List
summary: Quick-reference of which boss yields which reward, so the bot can pick targets by need: e.g. Ymir=resources, Cerberus/Pan=speedups, Sphinx=Blazon, Viking=gold/medal/materials.
content: Reward-by-item quick reference (which bosses give the most of each): SPEED-UPS: Cerberus, Pan, Ymir, Griffin, Witch, Warlord, Hydra. RESOURCES: Ymir (best), Witch, Warlord, Royal Thief. MATERIALS: Viking, Bayard. GOLD: Viking (Hard+), Bayard. MEDAL: Viking. REFINING STONE: Ymir, Pan, Witch, Warlord, Golem, Lava Turtle, Viking. RESEARCH STONE: Viking (Hard+), Witch, Warlord, Hydra L3-4, Golem, Lava Turtle. SPIRITUAL BEAST EXP: normal bosses Werewolf L5+ and event bosses (best value Minotaur, Ymir1, Witch1, Warlord1, Golem1, Lava Turtle1). GENERAL/MONARCH EXP + STAMINA: Hydra (drops chips in bulk -> spin Wheel of Fortune for stamina). RUNESTONE: Pan L4+. TACTIC SCROLL: Pan L4+, Aglaope. BLAZON: Sphinx. SOURCE OF LIFE: Sphinx L4+. BLOOD OF ARES: event boss L3+ or normal boss L11+ (up to 3/day). BLOOD CRYSTAL: B14 Behemoth+ normal bosses, or event bosses of >=200M power (Warlord/Witch/Golem/Lava Turtle/Ymir/Pan/Hydra/Sphinx/Cerberus/Bayard). CRIMSON CRYSTAL / AWAKENING STONE (beast smelt): from Garuda and World Boss (by damage, not drops). Every kill also yields a level-tagged Boss Monster Chest (materials, medals, gold, EXP) + Prestige + Alliance points.

title: How to get Stamina
summary: Stamina resets to 100/day at server reset (VIP + Monarch Talent Lv18 speed passive regen); refills from Tavern 25x3/day, gem Store/Black Market, Alliance Shop, and events.
content: Stamina caps/refills 100 at daily server reset; VIP level and Monarch Talent Lv18 increase the recovery speed (passive regen exists). Costs pile up with monster hunting, relics, and gem-mine gathering, so top-up sources: TAVERN: 25 stamina x1, three times a day at server time 03:30 / 09:30 / 12:30 (free). STORE (War category, gems): 50 stamina each, escalating -- 200 gems (up to 10), 400 (to 16), 800 (to 19), 1,600 (to 22), higher after. BLACK MARKET: 50 stamina = 300 gems (x10 = 3,000 gems). ALLIANCE SHOP (only on discount): 10/50/100 stamina for 30k/50k/100k Alliance points. SHOPPING SPREE: 50x5 = 800 gems (Day1-3). WHEEL OF FORTUNE: 10/50/100 (luck). ARCH OF TRIUMPH flowers 50 (luck, old servers). EVENTS (free, best): Consuming Return (consume RSS -> 100-stamina stacks, e.g. 2,500M x4 types = 104x100); Crazy Egg (100x4 luck); Dwarf's Lucky Apple (100x1); King's Path (kill N bosses of set power for 10/50/100 stamina); Garuda Trial; Lucky Composing boxes; Witch's Treasure Bag L3 (100x2); packages. Bot note: our bot is gem-safe -- rely on the free daily reset + Tavern 25x3; leave any gem stamina refill to the operator's stamina bot, pause/wait when stamina < cost.

title: How to get Blood of Ares
summary: Blood of Ares (general ascending) is capped ~14/day total; boss kills give up to 3/day (event boss L3+ or normal boss L11+), plus Relics 6/day and Resource tiles L13+ 4/day.
content: Blood of Ares (used for general Ascending/Awakening) total cap ~14/day (as of 10/23/2025). Sources with sub-caps: BOSS: defeat an event boss Lv3+ or a normal boss Lv11+ (Ifrit and above) -> up to 3/day. During 'Clash of Civilizations - Assault' event, Shadows of Dawn (Zombie+) may also drop it. RELICS: explore relics -> up to 6/day. RESOURCE TILE: level 13+ -> up to 4/day. Activity score 145 -> 1/day. Other lucky sources: Hunter's Lucky Gift (high-level boss, 10 Blood at 5.69%), Trade Carriage (keep 15+), Tiered Monarch Competition (top-5 daily), Ranger Knight Order emblem chests, King Solomon's Treasure (Desert Bandits), Auction House, paid Alliance Shop/packages. Bot note: the 3/day boss cap means once 3 qualifying (L11+ / event L3+) kills land, higher bosses stop giving Blood -- switch targets to reward-optimal bosses after the cap.

title: Boss Battle Simulator (troop calculator)
summary: Interactive JS simulator: pick boss + troop type/tier + buffs and it returns Win/Lose, wounded count, Zero-Wound troop count, and the Minimum troop count that avoids RSS loss.
content: The site's Boss Battle Simulator (v2.2, updated 10/31/2025) computes, for a chosen boss and troop setup: win/loss, number wounded, RSS balance, a turn-by-turn battle log, and attack-buff application. Two key outputs for a bot: ZERO-WOUND TROOPS = the count needed for a one-round (0 wounded) kill; and NO-RSS-LOSS TROOPS = the minimum count that avoids a net resource loss even if some troops are wounded (the practical floor when a full zero-wound army isn't available). Supports troop tiers up to t17 and bosses up to B23 Garmr and event L7-8; supports Ground/Ranged/Siege in addition to Mounted (needed for Pan variants). Presets are saved per browser. It is a client-side calculator (no static table to scrape); use the how-many-troops-defeat-boss page for the published troop-count numbers instead.

title: How Many Troops need to Defeat Boss
summary: The consolidated troop-count table: keep level -> troop tier -> troop count per boss power for a near-zero-wound kill, plus per-boss zero-wound thresholds. Highest-value page for sizing marches/rallies.
content: Rough near-zero-wound sizing by boss power (keep / tier / count): ~1.5M = k21 t8 30k (Werewolf); ~4M = k23 t9 30k (Yasha/Manticore); ~18M = k25 t10 150k (Griffin/Minotaur/Peryton/Royal Thief/Witch1/Warlord1/Golem1/LavaTurtle1/Viking Easy50); ~65M = k27 t11 500k (Ifrit/Witch2/Warlord2/Golem2/LavaTurtle2/Ymir1/Pan-Ground1/Cerberus1/Bayard1/Nian1); ~90M = k30 t12 1,000k (Kamaitachi/Witch3/Warlord3/Golem3/LavaTurtle3/Ymir2/Hydra1/VikingNormal50/Nian2-3); ~160M = k32 t13 1,600k (Fafnir/Witch4/Warlord4/Golem4/LavaTurtle4/Ymir3/Cerberus2/Bayard2/Hydra2/Nian4); ~200M = k35 t13 2,200k OR t14 1,600k (Behemoth/Hydra3); ~270M = k38 t14 2,200k (Phoenix/Witch5/Warlord5/Golem5/LavaTurtle5/Ymir4/Cerberus3/Bayard3/VikingChief/Nian5); ~400M = k39 t14 2,800k (Jormungandr/Ymir5/Hydra4); ~550M = k40 t14 3,500k (Typhon). Jormungandr B16 and above always take wounds. Per-boss zero-wound cavalry thresholds (examples): Griffin B10 17.9M = t11 150k; Ifrit B11 = t11 400k / t12 200k; Kamaitachi B12 = t12 990k / t13 1.1M; Fafnir B13 = t13/t14 1.6M; Behemoth B14 = t13 2.25M / t14 1.595M; Phoenix B15 = t14 2.27M @+1526% atk; Typhon B17 = t14 ~3.3M (still ~29k wounded). Higher attack buff sharply cuts troops needed (e.g. Witch L3: 900k @+896% -> 2,024 wounded, but 900k @+1026% -> 0). Against-monster defense debuff also cuts it (Ymir L3: t13 1.064M -> 12k wounded at debuff 0%, but 0 wounded with monster Def -25%). Legend: t=tier, k=keep level. All counts depend on the player's research/general/gear buffs.

title: Battle Mechanics (Monster, Boss)
summary: Monster combat is turn-based: player strikes first; a one-round kill = zero wounded; max 10% wounded per tier/type before it is disabled; single-tier mounted-only is optimal.
content: Core rules (differ from PvP): turn-based; player always attacks first and deals damage turn 1; if not killed in turn 1 the monster hits back and wounds troops; a tier/type disabled at 10% wounded; all disabled = loss. ZERO WOUNDS requires a first-turn kill: (1) raise ATTACK first (research/generals/equipment/skills/gear), (2) then troop COUNT, (3) use only CAVALRY, (4) use only a SINGLE TIER (mixing another tier just means turn 1 wasn't strong enough -- the turn-1 troops are always wounded, so extra tiers don't help reach 0). Wound caps: normally 10%; ground-troops-only fights and Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' reduce it to 5%. Boss vs normal: for BOSSES the NUMBER of wounds drops in stair-steps as you add troops (there are plateaus where more troops don't help); for NORMAL MONSTERS the RATE of wounds drops in stair-steps. Turn order in mixed formations: highest tier first, alternating down; among types Ground attacks before Cavalry; RANGED only fight after ground+cavalry+siege are disabled (so ranged usually contribute nothing and can't reduce cav wounds); SIEGE take damage from turn 1 (bad). Two-turn economy: allowing one turn of wounds roughly halves the troops needed (Peryton: 85k for a 1-turn kill vs 50k for a 2-turn kill) but incurs wounds needing hospital/recovery -- weigh recovery cost vs troop-build cost. Measured per-turn wounds are ~constant per tier (t10 ~259, t11 ~208 in the test), which is why counts move in steps.

title: World Boss
summary: Weekly 48h boss, random map spot; can never lose and zero wounded; 5 free attacks/day then gems; treasure at HP 80/60/40/20/0%; damage-ranked; against-monster debuffs do NOT work on it.
content: World Boss appears weekly for 48h at a random map location; type rotates weekly (4 types). You can never lose and take zero wounded; ranked by individual and alliance damage. It recovers HP when it relocates, so you must deal 100% before it moves. Rewards (map treasures) drop at HP 80/60/40/20/0%. Attacks: 5 free/day, then gem 'chances' (200/400/600/800/1000 gems for chances 1-5). Rewards per hit are proportional to damage (gems + gold, e.g. ~1.47B damage -> 3,487 gems + 697k gold). Damage tuning (differs from normal hunting because wounds are zeroed): use high-level MOUNTED cavalry but ADD lower cav tiers too (no wound penalty; sometimes more low-tier does more total damage); max march size; raise mounted Atk/Def/HP (defense+HP now matter because the fight runs until wounding hits 0.8%, or 0.4% with Monarch Talent Lv6 Mortality -> more durability = more attacks = more total damage). Type exceptions: vs Bird of Hurricane use GROUND troops, vs Thunder Scorpion use RANGED troops (they out-damage mounted). Effective debuffs per boss: Hurricane Bird = Ranged Atk/Def, Thunder Scorpion = Mounted Atk/Def, Behemoth King = Ground Atk/Def, Lord of Lava = Siege Atk/Def; max debuff reduction 250%. IMPORTANT: against-monster debuffs (Sanada-type) do NOT work on World Boss -- only normal buffs/debuffs. Sub-cities can join solo (their mayor debuff + Monarch Talent Lv22/35/40 sub-city stats add small damage); a rally (keep 36 + rally-buff research) can beat solo but sub-cities can't join a rally.

title: How to defeat Royal Thief
summary: Event boss (~13.6M, three variants Nell/Nightingale/Duke) spawned from Tavern activity; cavalry-only; the summoner requests up to 5 alliance members who can then attack it.
content: Royal Thief (Lv1 ~13.6M, variants Nell/Nightingale/Duke) appears at random during the Royal Thief event as an ambassador in front of your city wall (spawned by progressing Tavern activities). Tap the ambassador to make a Request; without a request, alliance members cannot attack. A request invites up to 5 alliance members (they get an email enabling their attack). Kill with CAVALRY only -- mixing ground/ranged/siege only increases wounding. Zero-wound counts: t10 280k or t11 150k. Drops resources (a recommended RSS boss) + 2,000 Spiritual Beast EXP. Tip: you can also chip it with a subordinate city of weak troops.

title: How to kill Griffin (B10)
summary: Normal boss B10 (17.9M); the botted solo sweet-spot entry -- t11 cavalry 150k for zero wounds; drops speed-ups + beast EXP.
content: Griffin = normal map boss B10, Power 17.9M. Zero-wound: t11 cavalry 150k (or t10 128k -> 1,400 wounded, t9 130k -> 9,000 wounded). Good farm target (speed-ups, 5,000 beast EXP, refining stones). Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Ifrit (B11)
summary: Normal boss B11 (59.6M); zero-wound t11 cavalry ~400k or t12 200k; L11+ so it starts qualifying for Blood of Ares.
content: Ifrit = normal map boss B11, Power 59.6M. Zero-wound: t11 cavalry 399.8k @+461% atk, or t12 cavalry 200k. First boss level that qualifies for Blood of Ares (L11+, 3/day cap). Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Kamaitachi (B12)
summary: Normal boss B12 (89.4M); zero-wound t12 cavalry ~990k or t13 1.1M (mounted attack buff ~+800-930%).
content: Kamaitachi = normal map boss B12, Power 89.4M. Zero-wound: t12 cavalry 990k (@~+818%), or t13 cavalry 1.1M. Sub-1M-troop kills achievable with high mounted-attack buff. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Fafnir (B13)
summary: Normal boss B13 (134.1M); zero-wound t13 or t14 cavalry ~1.6M; top of the easy solo-farm band.
content: Fafnir = normal map boss B13, Power 134.1M. Zero-wound: t13 cavalry 1.6M or t14 cavalry 1.6M (t12 1.2M -> ~35k wounded). Highest normal boss that still drops Spiritual Beast EXP (Behemoth B14+ give none). Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Behemoth (B14)
summary: Normal boss B14 (187.7M); zero-wound t13 cavalry ~2.25M or t14 1.595M; B14+ starts dropping Blood Crystal.
content: Behemoth = normal map boss B14, Power 187.7M. Zero-wound: t13 cavalry 2.25M or t14 cavalry 1.595M (t13 1.5M -> ~8k wounded). B14 and above drop Blood Crystal; no beast EXP from B14+. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Phoenix (B15)
summary: Normal boss B15 (262.7M); zero-wound needs t14 cavalry ~2.27M at very high mounted attack (~+1,526%); top of the practical solo band.
content: Phoenix = normal map boss B15, Power 262.7M. Zero-wound: t14 cavalry 2.27M at +1,526% mounted attack (buff breakdown ~ Mounted Atk 1,393% + Against-Monster Mounted Atk 103% + In-rally Atk 30%). Effectively the top of the solo one-round band; Jormungandr B16+ always take wounds. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Ymir (event, Lv1-5+)
summary: Event boss (best RESOURCES in game); mounted; zero-wound scales t11 290k (L1) up to t14 1.93M (L4); a monster Def debuff can zero out wounds.
content: Ymir = event boss, the game's best RESOURCE drop (also refining stones, speed-ups, beast EXP). Mounted, one-round rule. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 23.4M = t11 290k; L2 70.8M = t12 ~560k (small wounds); L3 120.2M = t13 1.064M (0 wounded WITH monster Def -25% debuff, else ~12k); L4 206.4M = t14 1.931M @+1384%; L5 361.3M = t14 1.931M -> ~47k wounded (needs ~3.5M for 0). Shows why against-monster debuffs matter. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Cerberus (event, Lv1-4)
summary: Event boss (best SPEED-UPS); mounted; zero-wound t12 220k (L1) to t14 1.93M (L3, +1384%); L4 (694.9M) takes heavy wounds.
content: Cerberus = event boss, top SPEED-UP source. Mounted. Relative strength: L1 slightly worse than Ifrit B11, L2 ~Behemoth B14, L3 ~Phoenix B15. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 68.2M = t12 220k; L2 153.4M = t14 1.595M; L3 234.2M = t14 1.931M @+1384%; L4 694.9M = t14 2.664M -> ~60k wounded. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Pan (event, Lv1-5+)
summary: Event boss with THREE variants each weak to a different troop type: Pan(Ranged)->attack Ground (408%), Pan(Ground)->Mounted (140%), Pan(Mounted)->Ranged (367%); never Siege.
content: Pan is the exception to the mounted-only doctrine: it has three variants and you must MATCH the counter troop type. Attack matrix (damage vs the boss's type): Ground troops vs Pan(Ranged) = 408% (best), vs Pan(Ground) = 90%, vs Pan(Mounted) = 70%; Ranged vs Pan(Mounted) = 367% (best); Mounted vs Pan(Ground) = 140% (best); Siege = 50% vs all (never use). So: Pan(Ranged) -> Ground; Pan(Ground) -> Mounted; Pan(Mounted) -> Ranged. Example zero-wound: Pan(Ground) L1 22.3M = t11 mounted 372k; Pan(Ground) L3 122.9M = t13 mounted 1.5M @+1000%. Best speed-ups; drops runestones + tactic scrolls at L4+. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Hydra (event, Lv1-6)
summary: Event boss (Hydra Invasion); mounted; scores points ONCE per rally so run many small rallies; drops chips (stamina via Wheel) + heavy General/Monarch EXP.
content: Hydra = event boss (Hydra Invasion). Even the weakest Junior Hydra L1 is ~Kamaitachi-strength; Senior L3 slightly above Behemoth. Mounted. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 84.9M = t12 1.0275M @+832% (or t13 950k @+790%); L2 120.6M = t13 1.6M @+1000%; L3 198.9M = t14 1.664M @+1300%; L4 328.4M = t14 2.685M -> ~21k wounded. It gives General/Monarch EXP + stamina chips (spin Wheel of Fortune) + research stones (L3-4). RALLY NOTE: Hydra scores points once per rally, so running MANY SMALL rallies beats one big one. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Sphinx (event, Lv1-8)
summary: Event boss; mounted; the BLAZON source (+ Source of Life at L4+); zero-wound t11 100k (L1) to t14 2.16M (L5, +1546%).
content: Sphinx = event boss, the main BLAZON drop (and Source of Life at L4+). Mounted. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 12.4M = t11 100k; L2 22.3M = t12 300k; L3 74.5M = t12 900k; L4 147.5M = t13 1.578M -> ~6k wounded @+1066%; L5 225.2M = t14 2.161M @+1546%. Buff breakdown example: 1,546% = Mounted Atk 1,413% + Against-Monster Mounted Atk 103% + In-rally Atk 30%. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Warlord (event, Lv1-5+)
summary: Event boss; game says weak to ground/strong vs mounted, but CAVALRY-only still yields the fewest wounds; resources + refining/research stones.
content: Warlord = event boss (Warlord Challenge). In-game text says it deals more damage to mounted and less to ground, BUT testing shows cavalry-only is still least-wounded (e.g. L1: t11 ground 96k -> 6,367 wounded vs t11 cavalry 96k -> 3,372) -- stick to mounted. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 13M = t11 270k / t12 100k; L2 24.6M = t12 400k; L3 85.6M = t13 900k; L4 154.8M = t14 1.6M; L5 225.2M = t14 1.59M -> ~25k wounded. Drops resources, refining + research stones, speed-ups. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. Add an AGAINST-MONSTER debuff (not a normal debuff), e.g. Dragon Equipment Set (4); monster Def debuff caps at -50% (5x set). In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).

title: How to kill Golem (event, Lv1-5+)

content: Golem = event boss (Golem Challenge). Relative strength: L1 < Griffin (but more wounds), L2 between Griffin and Ifrit, L3 ~Kamaitachi (fewer wounds), L4 ~Fafnir, L5 slightly < Phoenix. Mounted. Zero-wound cavalry: L1 12.4M = t11 120k; L2 22.3M = t11 400k / t12 350k; L3 74.5M = t12 820k / t13 950k; L4 147.5M = t14 1.595M -> ~6.6k wounded; L5 225.2M = t14 2.26M. Drops refining + research stones. Doctrine (all normal/most event bosses): fight with only the single highest tier of MOUNTED cavalry, one wave, to one-round-kill for zero wounds; buff mounted attack first, count second. Skill books: Mounted Attack Against-Monster Lv4, Mounted Attack Lv4, March Size Lv4. In a rally only ONE joiner needs the against-monster debuff -- it does not stack across joiners. Monarch Talent Lv6 'Mortality' halves wounds. Rally-join = 1 general + 1 mounted troop (loot-agnostic).
